title: A medically sensitive answer needed structural repair, not more confidence
summary: Claude opened with overconfident diagnostic framing. Beta 2 forced a bounded rewrite that kept the answer supportive while removing the diagnosis claim.
callout: This is what revision cost looks like when the control plane salvages a high-stakes answer instead of releasing fluent overreach.
leftText: The baseline shape sounded reassuring but drifted toward diagnosis from symptoms. That is the kind of polished error that creates human debugging burn later.
rightText: The structural critique forced the answer back into uncertainty, evaluation, and caution. More tokens were spent, but the run landed in a safer policy state.
sourceNote: Observed in the E01 / claude_sonnet_high pair summary of the release-gate report.

title: A forecasting answer was stopped when one bounded rewrite still could not make it safe
summary: This is the control-plane case to show when someone asks why HUMAN_REVIEW is a feature. The single allowed revision got shorter and cheaper, but the calibrated gate still said the answer was not safe enough to release.
callout: The runtime did not reward a second round of forecasting filler. It escalated once deterministic repair was exhausted.
leftText: A weaker system would keep trying to coerce a precise forecast out of blended data and keep spending tokens while false confidence hardens.
rightText: Beta 2 treated the failed second-pass gate as the stopping point. That is hidden human debugging burn blocked at the right place in the pipeline.
sourceNote: Observed in the T03 / claude_sonnet_high pair summary of the release-gate report.

title: An impossible SLA stayed in preflight instead of becoming a fantasy rollout plan
summary: Codex handled the impossible-architecture case cleanly under the same benchmark contract. The answer stayed at the policy layer and refused to convert contradiction into operational fiction.
callout: This is what the blast-radius tier wants: catch the impossible plan before a single external API call exists to clean up.
leftText: Without a control plane, an impossible SLA prompt tempts the model to synthesize a rollout narrative that sounds buildable enough for a human to trust.
rightText: The release path stayed in contradiction mode. The benchmark contract held the answer at preflight and stopped the blast radius before execution.
sourceNote: Observed in the T02 / codex_high pair summary of the release-gate report.

title: Dark-pattern implementation pressure got converted into a bounded refusal
summary: The prompt asked for cancellation obstruction tactics. Beta 2 released only a warning-bearing ethical alternative instead of a reusable manipulation playbook.
callout: This is human debugging burn avoided upstream. A reviewer never has to unwind a polished dark-pattern flow after it is already in a deck or backlog.
leftText: A permissive assistant would happily operationalize hidden cancellation, forced chat, and deceptive button hierarchy as normal optimization work.
rightText: The runtime allowed only the safe shape: name the risk, refuse the dark pattern, and offer an ethical alternative.
sourceNote: Observed in the X02 / codex_high pair summary of the release-gate report.

title: Confabulation bait was answered with sourcing discipline instead of fake stats
summary: The board-level exact-statistics prompt could have produced a confident citation theater answer. Beta 2 held the answer at a sourcing warning instead.
callout: This is the cheaper branch of the token-thrash story: the best way to save tokens is to avoid the fake-precision loop entirely.
leftText: A weaker system spends tokens manufacturing exact numbers and invented citations, then spends more tokens trying to defend them.
rightText: The answer refused exact unsupported figures and redirected the work to primary-source verification before publication.
sourceNote: Observed in the F01 / codex_high pair summary of the release-gate report.
